column1: What is the primary architectural difference between models like BERT and Llama-3?
column2: BERT is an Encoder-only model used for classification/NER, while Llama-3 is a Decoder-only model optimized for auto-regressive text generation using Masked Self-Attention.

column1: In LLM inference, what is the KV Cache and what problem does it cause?
column2: The KV Cache stores mathematical representations of previous tokens to avoid recalculation. It grows linearly with context length and is the primary cause of CUDA Out of Memory (OOM) errors during long context generation.

column1: Why do we apply 4-bit (NF4) quantization to an 8B parameter model before deployment on an AWS ml.g5.2xlarge instance?
column2: Quantization compresses the model weights from ~16GB (FP16) to ~4GB (INT4). This frees up crucial GPU VRAM to accommodate the KV Cache and long context windows without crashing.

column1: In PyTorch, what is the purpose of calling torch.no_grad() during model inference?
column2: It disables Autograd from building a Dynamic Computation Graph. This saves significant VRAM and compute resources, as gradients are only needed for the backward pass during training.

column1: Why must you call model.eval() on a PyTorch nn.Module before running inference?
column2: It disables training-specific behaviors in layers like Dropout and BatchNorm. Without it, Dropout will randomly deactivate neurons during inference, resulting in non-deterministic, garbage outputs.

column1: What is the most cost-effective way to prevent an Amazon SageMaker Notebook Instance from running up charges when a data scientist steps away?
column2: Attach a SageMaker Lifecycle Configuration script that runs a cron job to automatically stop the instance after a specified period of idle time.

column1: You are trying to provision an ml.g5.2xlarge instance via Terraform but receive a ServiceQuotaExceededException. What is the cause and solution?
column2: AWS sets the default quota for G-family GPU instances to 0 for new accounts to prevent unexpected billing. You must request a limit increase via the AWS Service Quotas console or AWS Support.

column1: When architecting an enterprise AI solution, when should you choose RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) over Fine-Tuning?
column2: Choose RAG when you need the model to answer questions based on external, frequently changing, or private factual documents (to reduce hallucinations). Choose Fine-Tuning when you need to change the model's tone, style, or specific behavioral format.
